meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on Lenz's law and the direction of induced currents
meta.description: Faraday's law, Lenz's law, motional EMF, and induced electric fields

patterns.name: Transformer Relations
patterns.recognition_signal: Two coils sharing magnetic flux, questions about voltage/current transformation
patterns.logic: Changing flux in primary induces voltage in secondary; power conserved in ideal transformer

pitfalls.name: DC transformer myth
pitfalls.description: Transformers only work with AC (changing flux)

worked_example.title: Step-Down Transformer
worked_example.problem_latex: Transformer has N_p = 1000, N_s = 100. Primary voltage is 120 V. Find secondary.

professor_tips: Lenz's law: induced effects oppose the change causing them
Three ways flux changes: changing B, changing A, changing angle
Motional EMF is just a special case of Faraday's law
Transformers require AC - they don't work with DC

confidence_checklist: I can calculate magnetic flux through a loop
I can apply Faraday's law to find induced EMF
I can use Lenz's law to find direction of induced current
